the-calculus-project-2: The Calculus Project - Creating a new path to success in advanced mathematics | A Path To Success In Advanced Math Advanced math adds up to future success. Learn about how you can partner with or support this impactful program making a difference in the lives of students of color. The Calculus Project - Creating a new path to success in advanced mathematics | A Path To Success In Advanced Math Skip to content Advanced math adds up to future success. DONATE PARTNER WITH US Completing advanced math courses in high school has a greater influence on whether students will graduate from college than any other factor. Our mission is to use research-supported strategies to increase the representation and success of low income students, as well as Black, Hispanic, and other students of color, in advanced mathematics. Learn More TCP is one of 150 local nonprofits that will share in $30 million through Cummings Foundation ’s major annual grants program. We were selected from a total of 715 applicants and we will receive a total of $150,000 over three years. The Problem Black and Hispanic people remain underrepresented in the science, technology, engineering and math (STEM) workforce. This holds them back from completing college and increasing their future earnings. Learn More The SOLUTION The Calculus Project works to solve the problem by working to increase the number of low income students of color engaged in advanced math courses. Learn More The Results 0 + Students Helped  since 2009 0 % of TCP students earned an A or B in the first semester of math 0 % of TCP students enroll in AP level math courses by their senior year. Learn More Jonathan Pierre graduated in our first cohort (class of 2020) and is a freshman at Yale. Watch Jonathan’s Building Bridges Speech Aiko White is the youngest student in TCPLA to pass the Massachusetts Test for Educator Licensure for Middle School Math. Read Aiko's Story DONATE NOW Become a TCP District View More Student Stories

Urban districts Health systems Community colleges Manufacturing Public sector Intermediaries What Craft handles A workforce program is a lot of moving parts. We hold them together. Most organizations do not fail to build a pathway for lack of will. They fail because the funding, the academic partner, the compliance, and the day-to-day tracking each live somewhere else. Craft brings them onto one platform. Braided funding We align the federal and state streams that pay for paid pathways and document the use of funds so the dollars hold up to audit. Named academic partner Related technical instruction is delivered by a national partner from day one, so you are not sourcing curriculum or accreditation alone. the-dyscalculia-training-research-instit: Home | Dyscalculia Training Dyscalculia is the math learning disability that affects millions of people around the world. The DTRI offers awareness, research, and training in this disorder. Home | Dyscalculia Training top of page DYSCALCULIA TRAINING & RESEARCH INSTITUTE The DTRI partners with schools to bring the bottom 30% of students to grade level in math.​ Join us! ​ Partner With Us Donate Now! Our mission is to maximize the academic achievement, personal growth, and professional opportunities of people who struggle with math learning difficulties. Our goal is to change the lived experience of people with dyscalculia, the math learning disorder. An Important Message from Dr. Wall Regarding the Canvas Hack and Future Plans (May 2026) ABOUT DYSCALCULIA Download a free brochure or infographic here Dyscalculia (diss-cal-COOL-ee-a) is a learning disability that affects how people learn, understand, and remember math. These students struggle with numeracy, fluency, and following steps and procedures, Even if a teacher is equipped with great interventions and trained in accommodations for students with learning differences, sometimes it still isn't enough. Why? Because dyscalculia is different! It doesn't mean that the teacher is ill-equipped or bad at their job - dyscalculia just isn't very well known yet; it isn't as "popular" as its cousin, dyslexia. Once you know how to support students properly, the results are immediate. The Dyscalculia Training and Research Institute is an organization dedicated to advancing research-based understanding of the disability, and to advocating for, and changing the lived experience of, people who have it. The organization is women-led and math-focused. The board of directors includes teachers, psychologists, and parents who understand dyscalculia. The leadership team is committed to expanding awareness and research about this neurodiverse Specific Learning Disorder. Join our mailing list Email Subscribe Thanks for subscribing! WHAT WE DO Meaningful Work Kindergarten to 6th Grade Recognizing dyscalculia Middle and High School Advanced math and dyscalculia College and Adults College, careers, and dyscalculia Want to know more about dyscalculia? Watch Dr. Wall's interview here: Contact Us The DTRI is a 501(c)3 non-profit organization based in Iowa. Our work is worldwide. EIN # 92-3250307 Submit bottom of page
